name: roadside-gallows
summary: A gallows or gibbet beside a road — a warning to travelers. Usually a single gibbet cage or three-post gallows on a raised earthen mound, with a notice board and a bench for the watcher.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: small, compact — a shrine of cruelty, not a plaza

accent.purpose: raised earth mound beneath the gallows

primary_palette.note: classic three-post gallows on a mound

story_prompts: recent execution — the body still hangs, the crowd gone
the gibbet's occupant is a notorious bandit; his gang plans to retrieve the body
dusk, empty, crows roosting — party passes through on a lonely road
a fresh corpse hangs, but the notice is for a different crime — wrong body?
the gallows is being used as a wildlife perch; no one has swung here in years

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: outdoor default; evening scenes 0.25-0.35 with cool tint

anti_patterns: invisible walls (iw) perimeter — the road continues past
never more than one centerpiece — gallows OR gibbet OR stocks, not a mix
scatter stays sparse — this is a threshold, not a gathering place
the notice-board should always be readable from the road approach
